_comment: Sentry custom dashboard config for AutonoMath / jpintel-mcp.
DRAFT — not applied. See docs/runbook/sentry_setup.md for the manual
- Sentry's exported dashboard JSON shape is undocumented and unstable;
this file uses a simplified internal schema. The runbook maps each
widget to the correct Dashboard UI controls.
- Queries use the Discover/EventsV2 query syntax where applicable, and
raw SQL where the data lives in our DB (Stripe MRR, backup manifest)
- A widget marked `data_source: stripe_metric` requires a custom Sentry
metric we emit from a cron job (see monitoring_gaps section).

dashboard.title: AutonoMath — Solo Ops Dashboard
dashboard.description: Single pane for the operator. 12 widgets covering revenue, traffic, errors, latency, infra, content quality.

widgets.title: MRR (¥)
widgets.explainer: Monthly recurring revenue, computed by scripts/cron/stripe_cost_alert.py from Stripe Reports API and emitted as a Sentry message tagged metric:billing.mrr_jpy. Comparison against 30d ago shows month-over-month delta. Widget renders as a big number with %-change annotation.

widgets.title: Active paying customers
widgets.explainer: Count of api_keys with active=1 AND stripe_subscription_id IS NOT NULL AND not in canceled state. Emitted from the same cron as widget #1. Anonymous users (50/month free) are NOT counted here. Drop = churn signal; sudden drop = billing webhook breakage signal.

widgets.title: 24h API request count by endpoint
widgets.explainer: Top 10 endpoints by hourly transaction count. Shows traffic shape (which routes are hot, where customers are spending the meter). Useful for spotting a sudden hot endpoint that might indicate a customer integration loop.

widgets.title: 24h error rate
widgets.explainer: Rolling 24h error rate at 10-min granularity. Three threshold lines visualize the SLO budget vs alert vs page lines from sentry_alert_rules.yml (rule api_5xx_rate).

widgets.title: Webhook health (Stripe + Postmark)
widgets.explainer: Two-column table: webhook source × log level. Row totals catch webhook-handler exceptions (R3 / rule webhook_handler_exception_rate). A normal day shows mostly INFO with a small WARN tail. ERROR rows >0 = investigate.

widgets.title: DB query latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
widgets.explainer: Three-line chart of DB transaction latencies. SQLite reads should be <50ms p95; >100ms means write contention (WAL checkpoint behind) or a missing index. p99 spike with stable p50 = tail latency / large query, not capacity issue.

widgets.title: Anonymous quota usage distribution
widgets.query: SELECT usage_count_bucket, COUNT(*) AS ip_count FROM ( SELECT CASE WHEN total <= 5 THEN '0-5' WHEN total <= 15 THEN '6-15' WHEN total <= 30 THEN '16-30' WHEN total <= 49 THEN '31-49' WHEN total = 50 THEN '50 (capped)' ELSE '50+ (illegal)' END AS usage_count_bucket FROM ( SELECT ip_hash, COUNT(*) AS total FROM anon_quota_log WHERE strftime('%Y-%m', request_at, 'localtime', '+9 hours') = strftime('%Y-%m', 'now', 'localtime', '+9 hours') GROUP BY ip_hash ) ) GROUP BY usage_count_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CASE usage_count_bucket WHEN '0-5' THEN 1 WHEN '6-15' THEN 2 WHEN '16-30' THEN 3 WHEN '31-49' THEN 4 WHEN '50 (capped)' THEN 5 ELSE 6 END)
widgets.explainer: Distribution of monthly anonymous-quota usage per IP (JST 月初 reset). The '50 (capped)' bucket size is the leading conversion signal — those IPs hit the wall and either churn or sign up for the paid ¥3/req tier. The '50+ (illegal)' bucket should be 0 — it indicates a quota-bypass bug.

widgets.title: Trial signup → paid conversion rate (rolling 7d)
widgets.query: WITH trial_signups AS (SELECT api_key_id, created_at FROM api_keys WHERE is_trial=1 AND created_at >= datetime('now', '-14 days')), paid_conversions AS (SELECT api_key_id FROM api_keys WHERE stripe_subscription_id IS NOT NULL AND first_paid_at IS NOT NULL AND first_paid_at - created_at < 7*86400) SELECT CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM paid_conversions) AS REAL) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trial_signups), 0) AS conversion_rate
widgets.explainer: Trial → paid within 7 days, rolling 14d window (= last 7 days of trials, allowing 7-day conversion window). Sub-1% = product-market fit signal too weak. >5% = healthy. Comparison vs 30d ago indicates trend.

widgets.title: Backup last success time
widgets.query: SELECT db_name, MAX(succeeded_at) AS last_success, ROUND((julianday('now') - julianday(MAX(succeeded_at))) * 24, 1) AS hours_since FROM backup_runs GROUP BY db_name
widgets.explainer: Two-row table: jpintel (target 1h) and autonomath (target 24h). Rule backup_integrity_failure pages on missing run; this widget gives advance visibility. Driven by analytics/backups.jsonl (manifest written by both backup crons).

widgets.title: License coverage (am_source.license filled %)
widgets.query: SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN license IS NOT NULL AND license != '' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS license_coverage_pct FROM am_source
widgets.explainer: Share of am_source rows with a non-null license string. Covered by /v1/am/health/deep::license_coverage check (warn at >1% null). Drop indicates a new ingest source skipped license-tagging — must fix before public-facing surface (景表法 + license-respect dignity).

widgets.title: GEO citation rate (last 7d)
widgets.query: SELECT date(request_at, 'localtime', '+9 hours') AS day_jst, 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN user_agent LIKE '%GPTBot%' OR user_agent LIKE '%ClaudeBot%' OR user_agent LIKE '%PerplexityBot%' OR user_agent LIKE '%Google-Extended%' OR user_agent LIKE '%CCBot%' OR user_agent LIKE '%anthropic-ai%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS llm_crawl_pct FROM anon_quota_log WHERE request_at >= datetime('now', '-7 days') GROUP BY day_jst ORDER BY day_jst
widgets.explainer: Share of anonymous traffic from known LLM crawlers. Per memory feedback_organic_only_no_ads we depend on GEO (generative engine optimization), not ads. A rising trend means LLM agents are citing/indexing us; flat at 0 means we're invisible to AI answer surfaces. Analytics-only widget — no action threshold.

widgets.title: Top 10 slowest queries (last 24h)
widgets.explainer: Top 10 DB query patterns by p95 latency over 24h. Pairs with widget #6 to identify which specific query is dragging the tail. SQLite FTS5 trigram queries are known offenders — see CLAUDE.md gotcha on the tokenizer fix.

monitoring_gaps_requiring_code.gap: Need a cron that calls Stripe Reports API and emits safe_capture_message(metric='billing.mrr_jpy', mrr_jpy=...) at level=info. Current scripts/cron/stripe_cost_alert.py only computes MTD fees, not MRR. Suggested: extend that script with --emit-mrr flag, or new scripts/cron/stripe_mrr_metric.py daily.
Same emitter as widget 1; query is local DB though, not Stripe. Cron computes COUNT and emits safe_capture_message(metric='billing.active_paying_customers', count=...).

Requires anon_quota_log table to exist with (ip_hash, request_at) rows. Verify in src/jpintel_mcp/api/_anon_ip_limit.py — log table may be in-memory / not persisted. If not persisted, add a daily aggregation cron writing to a histogram table.
monitoring_gaps_requiring_code.estimated_effort: verify first; if missing, ~2h to schema + cron

monitoring_gaps_requiring_code.gap: backup_runs table or the analytics/backups.jsonl manifest must be queryable. Both backup crons (backup_jpintel.py + backup_autonomath.py) already write to BACKUP_MANIFEST_PATH=analytics/backups.jsonl per disaster_recovery.md. Sentry's raw_sql data source can't read JSONL directly — need either (a) a tiny backup_runs SQLite table or (b) widget reads from a /v1/internal/backup_status endpoint.

Requires anon_quota_log to retain user_agent. Check current schema — if user_agent is dropped for privacy / quota efficiency, this widget cannot function. PII consideration: user_agent strings are not PII under common law but are under GDPR strict reading. Solo-ops decision: keep raw UA only for known crawler patterns, drop everything else after 7d.
monitoring_gaps_requiring_code.estimated_effort: 1h to add UA retention + 30 min Sentry widget config
